# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 952
- **Lines of code**: 313,754
- **Languages**: TypeScript (797), CSS (42), JSON (30), JavaScript (29), Markdown (25)
- **Zones**: 19 (19 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-01T03:15:44.179Z

## Architecture Zones

### Agent Safety Guard (`agent-safety-guard`)

Security and safety enforcement layer that validates agent commands and enforces execution policies.

- Files: 10
- Cohesion: 0.90, Coupling: 0.10
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts, packages/hench/src/guard/paths.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/agent-safety-guard/context.md`

### Agent Suggestions Store (`agent-suggestions-store`)

Specialized storage subsystem for managing agent execution suggestions and recommendations.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/agent-suggestions-store/context.md`

### Autonomous Agent Engine (`autonomous-agent-engine`)

Core autonomous execution engine that implements adaptive analysis, tool dispatch, and workflow completion.

- Files: 137
- Cohesion: 0.99, Coupling: 0.01
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/shell.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/autonomous-agent-engine/context.md`

### Dashboard Frontend (`dashboard-frontend`)

Core frontend components and viewer infrastructure powering the main web dashboard interface.

- Files: 182
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/components/logos.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/use-polling.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/performance/dom-update-gate.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/performance/graceful-degradation.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/performance/response-buffer-gate.ts (+3 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/dashboard-frontend/context.md`

### Graph Physics Engine (`graph-physics-engine`)

Provides physics simulation algorithms for interactive graph layout and visualization in the dashboard viewer.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.67, Coupling: 0.33
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/graph/physics.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/graph-physics-engine/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`


### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Inconsistent decomposition strategy - zone sizes range from 4 to 182 files indicating lack of uniform architectural principles [warning]
- Missing UI abstraction layer - heavy bidirectional coupling (16 crossings) between dashboard-frontend and web-middleware indicates missing interface boundary [warning]
- Multiple infrastructure zones exhibit bidirectional coupling with dashboard-frontend, violating layered architecture principle - infrastructure should serve UI, not depend on it [critical]
- Zone size distribution shows 68:1 ratio between largest and smallest zones (137 vs 2 files) indicating inconsistent decomposition strategy [warning]
- God function: handleHenchRoute in packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts calls 59 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Emergent layered architecture with clear dependency flow: orchestration → execution → domain → foundation [info]
- Hierarchical coupling distribution with UI zones <0.2 and infrastructure zones >0.3 demonstrates proper separation between business logic and system concerns [info]
- Mixed isolation strategies: some zones achieve perfect isolation (orchestration, suggestions store) while others use controlled bidirectional coupling (safety guard) [info]

### Relationships

- Dashboard-frontend zone acts as integration hub with high fan-out (9+8+2+2+2 imports) - consider interface extraction to reduce coupling complexity [warning]
- Hub-and-spoke pattern centered on dashboard-frontend creates single point of coordination but may introduce bottleneck risk for future scalability [warning]
- Optimal architectural layering between computational and temporal concerns with clean hub-and-spoke integration through dashboard frontend [info]
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 133 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (0.9) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (agent-safety-guard)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (agent-suggestions-store)
- High cohesion (0.99) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (autonomous-agent-engine)
- High cohesion (0.94) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (dashboard-frontend)
- Bidirectional coupling: "agent-safety-guard" ↔ "autonomous-agent-engine" (3+2 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "dashboard-frontend" ↔ "web-middleware" (9+7 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "performance-monitoring-infrastructure" ↔ "web-api-core" (2+2 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 182 files vs smallest with 2 — uneven decomposition [info]
- AI-assisted workflow integration throughout analysis and PRD management zones positions the platform for intelligent automation [info]
- Clear domain boundaries between orchestration, execution, safety, and infrastructure concerns enable independent package evolution [info]
- ... and 11 more

### Suggestions

- Consider controlled coupling with agent workflow - perfect isolation may prevent suggestions from informing agent decision-making [info] (agent-suggestions-store)
- Split oversized zone (137 files) into focused sub-domains: agent-analysis (adaptive, review, spin, stuck), agent-completion, agent-workflow, agent-schema [warning] (autonomous-agent-engine)
- Standardize naming convention - choose either behavioral naming (stuck, spin) or functional naming (completion, analysis) consistently throughout zone [info] (autonomous-agent-engine)
- Consider .tsx extension for React component files to follow conventional TypeScript+React patterns [info] (dashboard-frontend)
- Rename zone to 'dashboard-viewer' to align with actual file paths (packages/web/src/viewer/*) and resolve naming confusion [info] (dashboard-frontend)
- Define naming conventions for web zones: prefer shorter descriptive names over verbose compound names [info]
- Define optimal zone size guidelines - current variance (2-137 files) suggests inconsistent decomposition strategy across the codebase [info]
- Document test co-location convention or establish separate testing zones to clarify whether tests belong with implementation or in dedicated test boundaries [info]
- Establish consistent test isolation pattern - extract all test files from implementation zones to dedicated test zones [warning]
- Establish consistent zone naming convention - either semantic names (dashboard-frontend) or prefixed technical names (web-*) but not mixed approaches [info]
- ... and 26 more
